test.command: python3 -c 'import ast, os, sys; errors=[]; [errors.append((f,str(e))) for root,dirs,files in os.walk(".") for f in files if f.endswith(".py") for e in [None] if not (lambda: (ast.parse(open(os.path.join(root,f)).read()),None))()]; print(f"PASSED: all {len([f for root,dirs,files in os.walk(".") for f in files if f.endswith(".py")])} Python files have valid syntax")'
test.note: All 131 Python files pass syntax validation. Runtime tests run on remote GPU server via SSH/Docker - local machine has no ROCm. Tests were executed by each feature worker on root@192.168.1.198 via docker run with HIP_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0,1,2,3. Results documented in handoffs.

typecheck.note: No mypy/pytype installed locally. Syntax validated via ast.parse. No type errors evident from code review.

lint.note: No flake8/pylint installed locally. Code reviewed manually - follows project style conventions.

appliedUpdates.description: Added Sprint 3 M1 results section documenting: (1) hipStreamWaitEvent is host-non-blocking on gfx906/ROCm 7.1 - C dispatch already achieves maximum overlap without HIP graphs; (2) load-free-reload OOM pattern for MI50 when loading single-GPU + TP=4 reference together; (3) performance overhead breakdown: hipSetDevice ~2432 calls/token at ~3µs = ~7.3ms, event ops ~2048/token ~3.1ms, allreduce ~15ms; (4) D2D copy and stream sync overhead too small to be bottleneck under C dispatch (already hidden by async pipelining)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note to Testing & Validation Guidance: When loading both single-GPU reference and TP=4 engines in the same test, use the load-free-reload pattern to avoid OOM on MI50 (16GB VRAM). Pattern: load single-GPU → collect outputs → call engine.cleanup() + del engine → load TP=4. Loading both simultaneously causes OOM. See tests/test_qkv_sync_removal.py collect_single_gpu_reference() for reference implementation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: qkv-sync-elimination worker encountered OOM and solved it with this pattern. Worker skillFeedback noted this is not documented. Same pattern was adopted in direct-kv-cache-writes and allreduce-overlap-deepening tests. This is a systemic issue — every TP=4 correctness test needs it.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify correctness threshold in Correctness Thresholds section: The standard threshold is cosine sim >= 0.99. However, split-path implementations (e.g., split V GEMV where output goes to cache vs working buffer) may introduce FP16 ULP accumulation that drops to 0.98. In such cases, the reduced threshold (0.98) should be explicitly documented in the test file header and in the PR commit message. For bit-identical paths, 0.99 remains required.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: direct-kv-cache-writes lowered threshold to 0.98 for split V GEMV due to FP16 ULP accumulation. AGENTS.md currently states 'cosine sim >= 0.99 for decode' without caveat for split paths.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention note: Debug files (debug_*.py) generated during development should NOT be committed to the main tests/ directory. If needed for future reference, move them to a tests/debug/ subdirectory or delete before committing. The direct-kv-cache-writes feature committed 6 debug files (debug_20steps.py, debug_dims.py, debug_direct_kv.py, debug_direct_kv_cdispatch.py, debug_tp_comparison.py, debug_v_positions.py, debug_v_values.py) that clutter the tests/ directory.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: git show af3d217 --stat shows 6 debug_*.py files committed alongside test_direct_kv_write.py. These are development artifacts, not production tests.

rejectedObservations.observation: bench_*.py files placed in tests/ rather than bench/ directory is inconsistent
rejectedObservations.reason: This pattern (benchmark files in tests/) was already established before Sprint 3 (bench_tp4_sprint2.py, bench_optimization_baseline.py etc. all in tests/). Not a new issue introduced by these features. Changing now would require moving many files. Reject as pre-existing pattern.

rejectedObservations.observation: c_dispatch_v2.c duplicates most of c_dispatch.c, creating maintenance burden
rejectedObservations.reason: The project convention explicitly says 'Create NEW files (versioned names like _v2, _v3) rather than overwriting working code' per AGENTS.md. The duplication is intentional by design. Not a violation to flag.
